news.title: ASX extends losses; Origin Energy slumps as Atlas Arteria soars on $7b takeover bid
news.snippet: Energy stocks led the Australian sharemarket lower on Monday as investors reacted to developments in the Middle East.

news.title: Coles and Woolies need to eat some of the food price rise pain
news.snippet: Rising food prices are generally good news for supermarket profits. But there are good reasons why the two giants should absorb some of the food inflation.

news.title: Senator grills Woodside over huge political donations
news.snippet: Woodside executives have defended current gas tax settings after facing a parliamentary grilling over how much they donate to political parties.

news.title: ASX falls for fifth straight session
news.snippet: Australian shares have plunged for five consecutive sessions, with the market now facing its second straight week of losses amid global tensions.

news.title: Home-buying demand is falling off a cliff. Softer prices are a near-certainty
news.snippet: We may not be at a point of calling it a buyers’ market but this group is clearly recovering some of its bargaining power.

news.title: Dalaroo maps giant gold corridor in West African premier belt
news.snippet: Dalaroo Metals has wrapped up phase one soil sampling at its Gold Ridge prospect in Côte d’Ivoire, advancing toward maiden RC drilling along a 9.5km structural corridor.

news.title: Putin’s pain: The walls are closing in on Russia
news.snippet: The US may have gone soft on Russia, but the Europeans are getting more assertive, and they have also upset China in the process.

news.title: Paralympic champion shares emotional volatility after traumatic brain injury
Revolutionary plan to get Boomers out of their big homes
news.snippet: A new plan could help free up some of Australia’s 13 million empty bedrooms as the nation’s housing shortage deepens.

news.title: Massive update on Aussie fuel
news.snippet: Australia has struck a deal for another massive diesel shipment, as the Albanese government eyes one parched region.

news.title: ‘Basically sending someone unarmed into war’: The life-or-death decision Silke faces every day
news.snippet: Captain Silke Lehmköster grapples with the same question every day from her office in Germany.

news.title: Australia’s addiction to Amazon is set to blow past $5 billion
news.snippet: The numbers lay bare how rapidly Amazon is growing in Australia as it begins to make more money from ads, subscriptions and fees than it does selling goods.
